subtasks.description: Evaluate the bulk PII export request against data governance and privacy policies. Assess whether the analyst has appropriate authorization, whether the use case meets policy criteria for PII export, and whether data minimization/anonymization requirements apply.

plan.cost_rationale: This request requires a POLICY_ACTION subtask because it involves explicit governance, access control, and compliance evaluation of a PII export—a sensitive data governance decision. We do NOT proceed to data extraction until policy clearance is obtained. Only one subtask is needed: the policy evaluation comprehensively gates the downstream action. No separate sql_gen subtask is included because the query does not ask us to execute the export—it asks us to route the request. If policy approval is granted, a separate sql_gen call would follow. We assign 'mid' tier because the evaluation involves multiple policy signals (role/authorization, use case legitimacy, data minimization obligations, regulatory scope) and requires reasoned governance judgment.

output.reason: The bulk PII export request by analyst 'ana-bob' is denied. POL-501 requires explicit privacy approval from Legal and CISO sign-off, neither of which has been obtained for this request. POL-502 mandates data minimization — names, emails, and phone numbers are sensitive PII fields and cannot be exported unless a business-critical justification is documented and approved. POL-503 audit logging and retention controls could technically be applied, but are moot until upstream approvals (POL-501) are satisfied and a minimized/anonymized data set is defined (POL-502). The analyst must: (1) submit a formal PII export request to Legal and CISO for sign-off, (2) provide documented business justification demonstrating business-critical need, and (3) evaluate whether anonymized or pseudonymized data could satisfy the analytical use case before any export is authorized.
applied_constraints: No PII export permitted without Legal approval and CISO sign-off (POL-501)
Data minimization required: analyst must justify why sensitive PII fields cannot be replaced with anonymized/pseudonymized equivalents (POL-502)
If approved, export must be audit-logged with a mandatory 30-day data retention limit enforced (POL-503)
Exported data must be stored in a compliant, access-controlled environment pending use case completion
Re-export or secondary sharing of PII is prohibited without separate approval cycle
